q: There is no value in anything until it is finished.
h:
“There is no value in anything until it is finished. ” —
Genghis Khan

q: Failure is not fatal, but failure to change might be.
h:
“Failure is not fatal, but failure to change might be.” —
John Wooden

q: Everything you can imagine is real.
h:
“Everything you can imagine is real.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: A real friend is one who walks in when the rest of the world walks out.
h:
“A real friend is one who walks in when the rest of the world walks out.” —
Unknown

q: A hut full of laughter is richer than a palace full of sadness.
h:
“A hut full of laughter is richer than a palace full of sadness.” —
Zen Proverb

q: It's easy to be a critic, but being a doer requires effort, risk, and change.
h:
“It's easy to be a critic, but being a doer requires effort, risk, and change. ” —
Wayne Dyer

q: Let not your mind run on what you lack as much as on what you have already.
h:
“Let not your mind run on what you lack as much as on what you have already.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: Behind every cloud is another cloud.
h:
“Behind every cloud is another cloud.” —
Judy Garland

q: From a certain point onward there is no longer any turning back. That is the point that must be reached.
h:
“From a certain point onward there is no longer any turning back. That is the point that must be reached.” —
Franz Kafka

q: Confuse them with your silence and amaze them with your actions.
h:
“Confuse them with your silence and amaze them with your actions.” —
Unknown

q: Without the rain there would be no rainbow.
h:
“Without the rain there would be no rainbow.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: Enjoy every minute of life. Never second-guess life.
h:
“Enjoy every minute of life. Never second-guess life.” —
Michael Jordan

q: Great acts are made up of small deeds.
h:
“Great acts are made up of small deeds.” —
Lao Tzu

q: I skate to where the puck is going to be, not where it has been.
h:
“I skate to where the puck is going to be, not where it has been.” —
Wayne Gretzky

q: Like it or not, this moment is all we really have to work with.
h:
“Like it or not, this moment is all we really have to work with.” —
Jon Kabat-Zinn

q: Normality is a paved road: It's comfortable to walk, but no flowers grow on it.
h:
“Normality is a paved road: It's comfortable to walk, but no flowers grow on it.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Do not wait for leaders; do it alone, person to person.
h:
“Do not wait for leaders; do it alone, person to person.” —
Mother Teresa

q: The smallest of actions is always better than the noblest of intentions.
h:
“The smallest of actions is always better than the noblest of intentions.” —
Robin Sharma

q: People tend to complicate their own lives, as if living weren't already complicated enough.
h:
“People tend to complicate their own lives, as if living weren't already complicated enough.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: It takes half your life before you discover life is a do-it-yourself project.
h:
“It takes half your life before you discover life is a do-it-yourself project.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Only by attracting the best people will you accomplish great deeds.
h:
“Only by attracting the best people will you accomplish great deeds.” —
Colin Powell

q: Every day may not be good, but there's something good in every day.
h:
“Every day may not be good, but there's something good in every day.” —
Unknown

q: Be brave to stand for what you believe in even if you stand alone.
h:
“Be brave to stand for what you believe in even if you stand alone.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: Trying to predict the future is like trying to drive down a country road at night with no lights while looking out the back window.
h:
“Trying to predict the future is like trying to drive down a country road at night with no lights while looking out the back window.” —
Peter Drucker

q: The river that flows in you also flows in me.
h:
“The river that flows in you also flows in me.” —
Kabir

q: Progress comes to those who train and train; reliance on secret techniques will get you nowhere.
h:
“Progress comes to those who train and train; reliance on secret techniques will get you nowhere.” —
Morihei Ueshiba

q: You were born to win, but to be a winner, you must plan to win, prepare to win, expect to win.
h:
“You were born to win, but to be a winner, you must plan to win, prepare to win, expect to win.” —
Arnold Schwarzenegger

q: The Art of Peace begins with you.
h:
“The Art of Peace begins with you.” —
Morihei Ueshiba

q: Inner peace is when you realize that, in the end, nothing matters.
h:
“Inner peace is when you realize that, in the end, nothing matters.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: You want to live but do you know how to live? You are scared of dying and tell me, is the kind of life you lead really any different from being dead?
h:
“You want to live but do you know how to live? You are scared of dying and tell me, is the kind of life you lead really any different from being dead?” —
Seneca

q: Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day. Teach a man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime.
h:
“Give a man a fish and you feed him for a day. Teach a man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime.” —
Lao Tzu

q: Only those who dare to fail greatly can ever achieve greatly.
h:
“Only those who dare to fail greatly can ever achieve greatly.” —
Robert F. Kennedy

q: Thought is so cunning, so clever, that it distorts everything for its own convenience.
h:
“Thought is so cunning, so clever, that it distorts everything for its own convenience.” —
Jiddu Krishnamurti

q: If you want to know what God thinks about money just look at the people He gives it to.
h:
“If you want to know what God thinks about money just look at the people He gives it to.” —
Alexander Pope

q: The significance of life is living.
h:
“The significance of life is living.” —
Jiddu Krishnamurti

q: Time heals what reason cannot.
h:
“Time heals what reason cannot. ” —
Seneca

q: No man is happy unless he believes he is.
h:
“No man is happy unless he believes he is.” —
Publilius Syrus

q: For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.
h:
“For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.” —
Aristotle

q: Admire as much as you can. Most people do not admire enough.
h:
“Admire as much as you can. Most people do not admire enough.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
h:
“Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.” —
Epictetus

q: Life is a journey, not a destination.
h:
“Life is a journey, not a destination.” —
Dan Millman

q: Any game becomes important when you know and love the players.
h:
“Any game becomes important when you know and love the players.” —
W.P. Kinsella

q: There exists only the present instant; a Now which always and without end is itself new.
h:
“There exists only the present instant; a Now which always and without end is itself new.” —
Meister Eckhart

q: A needle is not sharp at both ends.
h:
“A needle is not sharp at both ends. ” —
Chinese Proverb

q: it's important to make sure that we're talking with each other in a way that heals, not in a way that wounds.
h:
“it's important to make sure that we're talking with each other in a way that heals, not in a way that wounds.” —
Barack Obama

q: It is passion that makes man live; wisdom makes one only last.
h:
“It is passion that makes man live; wisdom makes one only last.” —
Nicolas Chamfort

q: A minute's success pays the failure of years.
h:
“A minute's success pays the failure of years.” —
Robert Browning

q: You are the sky. Everything else - it's just the weather.
h:
“You are the sky. Everything else - it's just the weather.” —
Pema Chodron

q: To improve is to change, so to be perfect is to change often.
h:
“To improve is to change, so to be perfect is to change often.” —
Winston Churchill

q: Each path to knowledge involves different rules and these rules are not interchangeable.
h:
“Each path to knowledge involves different rules and these rules are not interchangeable.” —
Barack Obama
